results.notes: FWD C-body platform. Touring trim had 215/60R16.
E-body platform. Touring had sport tires.
FWD C-body. 60 Special had same specs. Brougham was RWD with 5x127.
RWD D-body. Different bolt pattern from FWD version.
RWD D-body. Different from FWD Fleetwood.
Final year, replaced by RWD Fleetwood.
Opel Omega platform. Different from other Cadillacs.
New W-body platform. Bolt pattern changed!
C-body platform. Ultra had wider tires.
MN12 platform. Different bolt pattern from Mark VII.
Final year of W-body Cutlass Supreme.
New N-body based Cutlass (separate from Supreme).
A-body platform. Some 1990 models may have 5x115.
G-body platform. Shared with Buick Riviera/Park Avenue.
GMT330 platform (S-10 Blazer based). 5x4.75 bolt pattern.
U-body minivan platform. GL/GLS had wider tires.

notes: Cadillac Fleetwood changed from FWD (5x115) to RWD (5x127) in 1993
Cadillac Catera uses 5x110 (Opel platform) - different from other Cadillacs
Buick Century changed from 5x100 to 5x115 in 1997 with W-body platform
Buick Roadmaster uses 5x127 (B-body, shared with Chevy Caprice)
Lincoln Mark VII uses 5x114.3, Mark VIII uses 5x108 - bolt pattern change
Oldsmobile Cutlass Ciera uses 5x100 (A-body), Cutlass Supreme uses 5x115 (W-body)
Hub bores: 70.3mm for most GM luxury FWD, 78.3mm for GM RWD B/D-body, 57.1mm for A-body/N-body, 63.4mm for Ford/Lincoln
